disclosure_items.clinical_fact: An adult found unresponsive and not rousable to voice or being shaken
trigger_question_examples: Is he responding to you at all?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No — he's completely out of it. I've shouted and shaken him and he doesn't react at all.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Very slow, shallow breathing with long pauses (reduced respiratory effort)
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: He's barely breathing — just a few slow shallow breaths with long gaps in between.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Blue/dusky lips and fingertips — visible cyanosis of a poorly oxygenated patient
trigger_question_examples: What colour are his lips and fingers?
Does his skin look blue anywhere?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: His lips and fingertips are going blue, and his face is really pale.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: The black centres of the eyes appear as tiny pinpoints (video-observable miosis)
trigger_question_examples: Can you look at his eyes — how big are the black centres?
Are his pupils large or very small?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Now that you say it — the black centres of his eyes are tiny, like little pinpoints.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Empty packets of a strong painkiller (oxycodone) and a sedative (diazepam) and drug paraphernalia found beside him
trigger_question_examples: Is there anything near him — pill packets, bottles?
Do you know if he took anything?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: There are empty packets of his oxycodone and some diazepam right next to him, and some other bits and pieces.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Known prior difficulties with painkiller and sedative use, using again recently
trigger_question_examples: Has he had trouble with drugs or medicines before?
Is there any history of substance use?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Yes — he's had problems with drugs for years, and I know he's been using again lately.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Skin feels cool and clammy to the touch
trigger_question_examples: What does his skin feel like?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: His skin is cool and clammy — a bit sweaty and cold at the same time.
